Party_Details.Name: Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd was set up by the third party.
Tan Chew Sim was appointed a director of Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd.
Zolton Techs provided maintenance services to 7-Eleven's mashed potato machines.
7-Eleven's manager discussed with the defendant about supplying new mashed potato machines.
A purchase order for 100 machines was sent by 7-Eleven to the defendant, addressed to Zolton Techs.
Mashed potato machines were supplied to 7-Eleven by SCM, a company controlled by the defendant.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendant was legally responsible for diverting a business opportunity from the plaintiff to SCM.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must not contravene their management obligations by diverting corporate opportunities for personal gain without consent.
Party_Details.Application: The plaintiff stated that the defendant diverted the opportunity without consent. The defendant argued that the third party consented to the arrangement.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the defendant had the third party's consent to supply machines through SCM, and thus did not contravene her management obligations.

Party_Details.Name: Tan Chew Sim

Facts.Text: Tan Chew Sim was appointed a director of Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd.
Tan Chew Sim married the third party, Chow Hoo Siong.
A purchase order for 100 machines was sent by 7-Eleven to the defendant, addressed to Zolton Techs.
Mashed potato machines were supplied to 7-Eleven by SCM, a company controlled by the defendant.
Lim from 7-Eleven requested the defendant to arrange installation and demonstration of a mashed potato machine.
The defendant incurred personal expenses on the plaintiff's corporate credit card.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendant was legally responsible for personal expenses charged to the plaintiff's corporate credit card.
Party_Details.Rule: A director may incur personal expenses on corporate accounts only with explicit consent or ratification by the company.
Party_Details.Application: The defendant stated that there was an understanding with the third party allowing such expenses. The plaintiff argued no such agreement existed.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that there was an understanding between the defendant and the third party allowing personal expenses on the corporate credit card.

Party_Details.Name: Tan Chew Sim

Facts.Text: An oral agreement was made for the defendant to receive a 5% incentive bonus based on the plaintiff's revenue.
The defendant claimed unpaid salary and bonuses from the plaintiff.
The defendant obtained an expedited personal protection order against the third party.
The defendant's employment with the plaintiff was terminated.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendant is entitled to salary in lieu of notice and a 5% incentive bonus.
Party_Details.Rule: An employee is entitled to salary in lieu of notice and agreed bonuses unless validly terminated for misconduct.
Party_Details.Application: The defendant stated she was entitled to these payments based on prior agreements. The plaintiff argued the defendant was not entitled due to stated misconduct.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found the defendant was entitled to the 5% incentive bonus and salary in lieu of notice, but not for accrued leave.

Party_Details.Name: Zolton Techs Singapore Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: An oral agreement was made for the defendant to receive a 5% incentive bonus based on the plaintiff's revenue.
The defendant claimed unpaid salary and bonuses from the plaintiff.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the company is legally responsible to pay the defendant's claimed salary and bonuses.
Party_Details.Rule: A company is bound to honor employment agreements unless validly contested.
Party_Details.Application: The plaintiff argued the defendant was not entitled to the claimed amounts due to stated misconduct. The defendant stated the claims were based on valid agreements.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found the company liable for the 5% incentive bonus and salary in lieu of notice, but not for accrued leave.
